data.title: Add idempotency guard to apply-attribution-trailers.sh to prevent duplicate trailer injection
data.description:
Gap analysis finding: T8 documents an orchestrator-level call to apply-attribution-trailers.sh from COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md, while T10 wires the same script into a git hook. If both execute during the same commit, git interpret-trailers will append duplicate trailer lines (DSO-Agent: dso:sprint appearing twice), since read_and_deduplicate only deduplicates JSONL entries, not already-present trailers in the commit message.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given the commit message already contains at least one DSO-Agent, DSO-Skill, or DSO-Model trailer, when apply-attribution-trailers.sh runs, then no duplicate trailers are appended and exit code is 0
- The idempotency check must scan the commit message file before calling git interpret-trailers; if all would-be trailers are already present, skip the git interpret-trailers call entirely
- The implementation must not break the primary injection path (first invocation on a message with no existing DSO trailers still injects all trailers correctly)

TDD requirement: Write test_skips_injection_when_trailers_already_present as a RED test first. Given a commit message file that already contains 'DSO-Agent: dso:sprint', when apply-attribution-trailers.sh runs with a JSONL file containing the same agent entry, then the commit message is unchanged (no duplicate trailer appended).

This task resolves the T8/T10 dual-wiring conflict regardless of which mechanism ends up being primary.
